identificationModule.briefTitle: ShenFu Injection for Myocardial Protection in Patients With Acute ST Segment Elevation Myocardial Infarction
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Effect of Shenfu Injection on Reperfusion Injury in Patients With Acute ST Segment Elevation Myocardial Infarction After Primary Percutaneous Coronary Intervention: A Pilot Trial

collaborators.name: China Resources Sanjiu Medical & Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd.

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to evaluate whether perioperative use of Shenfu Injection could reduce myocardial injury (enzymatic infarct size and infarct volume according to cardiac magnetic resonance imaging) in patients with STEMI after primary PCI

armGroups.label: Shenfu Injection
armGroups.description: 80ml Shenfu Injection + 70ml 5% glucose injection), ivdrip, 30 minutes before PCI and 1-5 days (once a day) after PCI (6 times in total). Drug titration time should be no less than 30 minutes.

armGroups.label: 5% Glucose Injection
armGroups.description: 150 ml 5% glucose injection, ivdrip, 30 minutes before PCI and 1-5 days (once a day) after PCI (6 times in total). Drug titration time should be no less than 30 minutes.

primaryOutcomes.description: Infarct size was assessed by measuring the area under the curve (AUC) of creatine kinase isoenzyme (CK-MB) within 72 hours after PCI and by performing cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) imaging at 4±1days after PCI.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: immediately after admission (0 hour), and 6, 12, 18, 24, 48, and 72 hours after PCI, as well as 4±1days after PCI

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: immediately after admission (0 hour), and 6, 12, 18, 24, 48, and 72 hours after PCI

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Peak value of CK-MB and cTnI
ST segment resolution (%) according to ECG
Left ventricular end-diastolic diameter (LVEDD), left ventricular end-systolic diameter (LVESD), and Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF)

Main adverse cardiovascular and cerebrovascular events (MACCE, including death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, target vessel revascularization, stroke, new-onset severe heart failure, re-hospitalization for heart failure)

locations.facility: Beijing Anzhen Hospital, Capital Medical University

references.citation: Wang X, Miao H, Yan Y, Guo R, Gong W, He Y, Wang H, Ma X, Nie S. Effect of Shenfu Injection on Reperfusion Injury in Patients Undergoing Primary Percutaneous Coronary Intervention for ST Segment Elevation Myocardial Infarction: A Pilot Randomized Clinical Trial. Front Cardiovasc Med. 2021 Dec 3;8:736526. doi: 10.3389/fcvm.2021.736526. eCollection 2021.
